About.Name: Jake R Graham
About.AboutText: I am a dedicated and hard-working Electrical Engineering student at Seattle Pacific University.

Experience.ExperienceText: I have an aptitude for mathematics, computer science, and engineering. Because of these, I was highly encouraged throughout my life to pursue an engineering degree. I initially resisted the idea when I graduated from High School because I didn't particularly enjoy either the experience or the classes, but after taking a year off I was encouraged to continue my education, and to my delight, I found that all of the interesting classes I had been wanting were found in college. Since that discovery, I have been working and studying hard to get an Electrical Engineering degree in order to become an Embedded Engineer, where I hope to use my math, computer science, and engineering skills to create new solutions for complex problems.
Experience.EducationText: Use this to add some narrative to your education section

Sallen-Key Filter.Title: 10 kHz Sallen-Key Filter
Sallen-Key Filter.Desc:
A project I completed in my Circuits II class was to design a 10kHz Bandpass Sallen-Key filter whose output was put into a Peak Detector and Comparator,
so that a signal was output from the comparator only when a 10kHz signal was put into the filter. I designed a 4-stage filter with a Quality factor of 4 for each stage for a maximum amount of attenuation,
and I used a voltage divider at the input of the circuit to reduce the filter's gain by a factor of 1000V/V. Below are pictures of the filter, the filter schematic, and a Frequency-Response Analysis Bode Plot of the filter.
